desc:
This ledge has a commanding view of the trails below. The cliff face to the east
has a large door sealing off what appears to be a cave. To the west, a steep
trail leads down to the other, larger, trails. A cave opens up to the east.
name: High Ledge

desc:
This cavern is wide at the centre but narrows to the east and north making it an
ideal place for defence. A large gate has been set into the western rockwall,
improving the chances to hold this cavern against intruders. Fresh markings in
the eastern stonewall surround a doorway.
name: Gate Cave

desc:
This is a square artificial cavern with fresh marks on the stone. It is very
tidy and quite warm. A bed, two chairs and some shelves make up the furniture.
The only exit is back through the door.
name: Secluded Cave

desc:
The rocky tunnel bends to and fro, stretching both to the north and to the
south. The southmost end narrows and turns steeply downwards in the same place,
making further travel in that direction impossible for any beast larger than a
rat. Near this dip in the tunnel a metal ladder has been set into the grey rock
wall, leading upwards into a cave.
name: Winding Tunnel

desc:
This is a rather small cave, circular in shape and with a diameter slightly less
than thirty feet. The black rock walls are almost completely clad in blueish ice
while numerous icicles and stalactites hang down from the cavern ceiling. Two
metal ladders disturb the natural air of this cave, one leading down into a
narrow hole and the other leading up to another cavern.
name: Icy Cave

desc:
The floor of this rocky tunnel is lined with numerous long and irregular rifts.
Several massive stalactites hang from the ceiling high above. In a few spots,
the otherwise grey rock is graced with the sparkle of some other, more
metal-rich, mineral. The tunnel extends to the north and to the south.
name: Natural Tunnel

desc:
Large metal hooks have been set into the wall at various heights from the
ground. The rock walls around some of these hooks are stained with dried blood.
Some weird machines are standing in the middle of the room, probably constructed
with one thing in mind - to make people talk. Fresh blood has been splattered on
most of the southern wall.
name: Torture Chamber

desc:
The air in here is very moist, and small puddles of water cover the stony floor.
A large hole has been barred off with steel bars in the eastern wall. Tiny piles
of small stones cover the floor in the vicinity of the bars. A large hole in the
southern wall seems to lead to another cavern, and a hole in the floor is the
beginning of a tunnel.
name: Mustering Hall

desc:
This tunnel has a very irregular floor, as if some unnatural beast had clawed
rifts into it. The rock is almost black, and stalactites hang from the ceiling.
Small steps have been carved into the western solid rock wall, allowing an easy
climb into the extending tunnel. A massive gate is set into the southern wall.
The eastern wall is covered with cracks and crevices, scars from an ancient
rockslide.
name: Dried-up Underground Stream

desc:
This is a small chamber created by a rockslide not to long ago. The air is moist
and the faint dripping of water can be heard. A bed has been placed in one
corner of the cave, it looks like its been used recently. Small mushrooms cover
most of the floor, many of them crushed.
name: Dark Moist Cave

desc:
Wooden targets resembling elves and orcs have been lined up on the far southern
side of this square cavern. Cuts and markings have been slashed into the rock
walls, most likely by weapons missing their targets or glancing off shields. A
few fresh red stains on the stone floor indicate that combat with a bloody
outcome occurred here recently.
name: Battle Training Cave

desc:
Four crude wooden tables have been situated in the middle of this cave, benches
placed around them to allow for a brief rest during meals. Glowing coals placed
in a concavity in the ground warms a thin stone slab making it possible to cook
food on it. Large cupboards cover the eastern wall completely, most likely used
to store huge amount of rations.
name: Mess Hall and Kitchen

desc:
This tunnel is very narrow. Sharp rocks are protruding from the walls,
threatening to pierce everything they come in contact with. A light draught can
be felt from the northern tunnel in this t-shaped junction.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
This large cave seems to have been crafted by the powers of nature, ages ago.
Close to the eastern wall, a wooden platform has been constructed. Exits lead
deeper into the mountain to both the north and the south. The air seems somewhat
warmer to the north.
name: Gathering Hall

desc:
The warm floor of this ledge slopes slightly inwards to the middle. Black trails
of smoke slowly rise towards the ceiling high above, adding to the darkness of
this cave. To the south a tunnel slopes sharply downward into the depths of the
mountain. Looking down, one can see a large forge cave below.
name: Warm Ledge

desc:
A well has been built upon a stone dais in the middle of this small cavern,
allowing access to water in case of a siege. The western wall of this cavern has
been slightly blackened around a jagged opening leading deeper into the
mountain. A constant jet of hot air flows into this chamber from this opening,
warming the surroundings. The cave grows larger to the south.
name: Well Hall

desc:
A crude stone forge sits in a depression of the uneven floor of this cavern,
simple tools and firewood piled by its side. The fire in the forge blazes, its
occasional flares turning the walls blood-red before letting them fade to black
again. The broken ceiling of the cavern is supported by a few blackened rafters
which can barely be seen through the smoke.
name: Forge Cave

desc:
The tunnel is very finely crafted, with smooth walls and floor. It ends rather
abruptly to the north, where a large cave-in has left only a small hole in the
wall. A pair of solid iron rails lead into the low tunnel. To the south, the
tunnel opens up to a large cave. Next to the tunnel entrance a huge wheel has
been mounted into the wall, connecting to an extremely thick metal wire.
name: Partially Caved-in Mine Tunnel

desc:
Mushrooms cover the floor and most of the walls of this narrow, steep tunnel.
The tunnel rises to the north into what looks like a crossing. The air in here
is very moist and seems to attach itself to everything it comes in contact with.
name: Steep Tunnel

desc:
Here the tunnel branches into every compass direction except west. This tunnel
is very narrow, but clearly not crafted by nature. It was probably dug to
connect two places with each other.
name: Branching Tunnels

desc:
The tunnel slopes from the west into the mountain to the south, where it seems
to become wider. Faint metallic sounds can ocasionally be heard from that
direction.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
A faint draft can be felt from the western exit in this tunnel going east-west.
The tunnel is exceptionally straight and does not look like it was created by
the forces of nature.
name: Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel narrows and starts sloping upwards towards the surface of the
mountain. The walls here have a dark red hue originating from some kind of
mineral packed into them. Small piles of bat-droppings make progress upwards
somewhat harder.
name: Tunnel

desc:
New markings on the rock forming this tunnel indicate that it was carved out of
this massive mountain recently. A trapdoor has been set into the roof at the
higher end of this steeply sloping tunnel.
name: Steep Tunnel

desc:
You are in the beginning of a large cave that extends far to the south. It is
quite cold in here, and the water dripping from the ceiling quickly turns to ice
as it is exposed to the frigid air. You can't see very far to the south...the
cloud of steam and vapors from your breath obscures your vision.
name: Freezing Cave

desc:
You are in a crack in the mountains that leads back to the west, or if you are
daring, down further into the mountain. It is quite cold here, and you can see
ice forming on the walls and ceiling, the beginnings of a complex structure of
ice stalagmites and stalagtites.
name: Inside the Cold Crack

desc:
The walls in this place seem to be made from glass, but one painful touch lets
you know that it is in fact ice. As you look around, you see bones that appear
to be from a game animal, and you wonder what kind of beast could live in this
desolate place.
name: Deep inside the Frozen Cave

desc:
As you step into this large cave, your breath comes out in steamy clouds,
fogging your view of what lies ahead. However, when you hear a loud growling
from behind a ice stalagmite, you fear that you have made a mistake coming to
this frozen lair.
name: Ice-Demon's Lair

desc:
Thanks to your keen intelligence and eyesight, you managed to find the crack in
the mountain face. It is a very tight squeeze, but you can turn sideways and
continue on to the east. As you look to continue, a strong blast of frigid air
hits you from the east, and you wonder why you suddenly got very cold.
name: Crack in the Mountain Wall

desc:
*WHEW* You have finally made it to the top. It was definitely worth the climb,
because the view from here is amazing. You can see a great deal of Arda from
your position here. You really would like to stay up here much longer, but the
thin air and high winds make it difficult to stay here and be safe.
name: Top of the Misty Mountains

desc:
Inside a hole stained with blood, you find a large cave. It shows signs of
habitation, with strewn bones and mouldy pieces of something even an orc will
not eat. Some chairs are put around a large table, which is covered with bones
and other dead parts of animals or human beings. However, this cave does not
seem like others you have seen in this area. Shields marked with strange
pictures and walls with strange colours mark this cave as an alien one. What
once must have been an opening in the northern wall is now completely covered
with debris. The only way out is to the west.
name: Collapsed Inner Cave

desc:
You go on, and soon you feel lost. You make a turn and walk back, looking for
the path you left. You are pretty sure that you are walking the exact same route
that lead you to this place. Still, the area you are in seems unfamiliar. There
is no sign of the path. Did you take the wrong turn somewhere? Hmm. The way back
to he path must be around the hill east of here. It has to be.
name: Soon Lost

desc:
You were wrong! You find no path. You are far away from it and lost. Your only
option is to walk on. So you do, firmly believing that you have to run into a
path eventually.
name: The Hill

desc:
You walk a while. The hope of finding the path grows smaller. You are far and
deep into the Mountains. Snow covers the ground, and there is a strong wind.
Snow drifts away by the wind, making the outlines of the landscape blurry. The
wind is cold! Your cheeks are aching. You go at a good pace; your body and feet
are still warm.
name: Mountains

desc:
You have no idea of where you are. Your range of vision is very limited. The
wind is very strong. Snow is drifting in the wind at great speed. Tiny ice
crystals constantly bombard your face. It feels like someone pricks your face
with hundreds of small needles every second. You can barely see anything; you
are forced to keep your eyes closed.
name: Lost in a Blizzard

desc:
The wind makes walking very troublesome. At times you have to sit down, or else
you would be blown away. The ground is still covered by snow. It is dry and
hard, almost like ice, polished by days of strong wind. Your strength drains
rapidly. If you do not find any shelter quickly, you risk severe frostbite.
name: Lost in a Blizzard

desc:
You can't go any further in these extreme weather conditions. Your face already
suffers from frostbite. It's numb with cold. So are your fingers. Some you can't
move. For a while, they have had a deep purple colour and aching. Now they feel
warm, but large areas are of a white colour, and small black spots appear. You
doubt that is a good sign. It really is very cold, it's not only the wind making
it feel like it. The snow is dry and crispy, squeaking beneath your feet. Any
liquid from your mouth, nose or eyes freezes instantly, and even breathing is
troublesome. You'll have to find shelter fast. These conditions might kill you
within the end of the day, no matter how hard you struggle against them.
name: A Very Cold Place

desc:
You found a snowdrift well suited for making a cave. Even though you hardly can
do anything with your frozen fingers and the fact that you have no equipment
made for digging snow caves, you manage to dig a tunnel some metres into the
snowdrift. At the end, you widen it to make it possible to sit and turn yourself
around. You are now protected by metres of packed snow, a perfect protection
from the blizzard outside. The temperature inside your cave is really
comfortable, just below the freezing point and most important: no wind, of
course. The frostbite seems to heal slowly, and you slowly regain some of your
strength. You stay here weatherbound, waiting for better weather conditions.
name: Snow Cave

desc:
You leave the cave behind you and feel a cold breeze in your face. This is a
snowfield high up in the Mountains, which gives you a good overlook over the
surroundings. It is hard to walk here, since every step you take makes you sink
down into the snow, and you have to struggle hard to get anywhere. As you walk
on, you suddenly see the the path you left below you.
name: Outside the Cave

desc:
It really is the path you left before! You have been pretty close to it all the
time, but no way you would ever find it during the blizzard. You walk back to
the path just a bit below you. Sometimes boulders comes galloping down besides
you, maybe let loose by mid-day sun on the snow above you.
name: By the Trail

desc:
The air here is warm and dry, leaving those who breathe it with a parched
throat. A heavy stench of decomposing flesh originates from the remains of
half-eaten small animals and torn-off limbs hanging from chains that are nailed
to the walls. The ground and the cavern walls are unusually jagged, and there
seems to be no spot for a comfortable rest.
name: Inside a Cell

desc:
During the march of time this shallow cavern has been smoothened by decades of
wind and rain. The ceiling slants sharply towards the sky, leaving minor
protection against the brute force of nature. The polished floor has been
ruptured close to the inner cavewall to establish a place for the trapdoor
leading down into the mountain. At the centre of the cave, rather close to the
trapdoor, a large bell has been mounted.
name: Cave Overlooking a Trail

desc:
Frost and ice line the sides and floor of this cavern, having been blown in by
the wind from the smoothed cave entrance to the south. The constant wear of the
elements has caused a cascade of cracks to form in the domed ceiling where an
enormous ring of icicles now hang; crowning the centre of the cavern.
name: Frost-rimmed Cavern

desc:
Snow and ice, freezing cold. Winds whipping the mountains with hail as hard as
steel. The cold itself seems to have a soul, filled with hatred for all life.
There is a dwelling in this freezing inferno, the home of the Zaugurz orcs. This
is where the Zaugurz orcs rest, to return later to their hunts and war.
name: Frozen North

desc:
Deep inside the frozen, icy lands of the North, this cave has been dug as a
shelter by the hands of the Zaugurz tribe. Now it is used solely by those
battle-tested Zaugurz who have need of rest and recovery before thrusting forth
once more into battle.
name: Frozen Cave

desc:
The cavern here is small and the air seems to freeze your lungs. Most of the
small cavern within the shimmering ice is taken by a sky-blue pool of water. It
is not frozen despite the temperature here feeling colder than anything else.
Deep down, a faint light of silvery blue seems to shine, yet even that little
bit that reaches up here promises a cold that lies even beyond the air's
temperature.
name: The Pool of Ice

desc:
Snow and ice, freezing cold. Winds whipping the mountains with hail as hard as
steel. The cold itself seems to have a soul, filled with hatred for all life.
There is a dwelling in this freezing inferno, the home of the Zaugurz orcs. In
one of these caves, the Zaugurz have discovered an old and very long mining
tunnel, leading far to the south. The brave ones daring to fight for glory enter
a mine cart and race through the mountain to a new world. The ones braver yet,
risk the monstrous blizzards and thundering avalanches of the Misty Mountains
and make the journey south on foot.
name: The Frozen North
